title: Humboltia dura
intro: Humboldtia dura, also known as the devil's tree, is a plant species native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India, characterized by its unique, thorny appearance and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia dura include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health issues.
taxonomy: Humboltia dura is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This classification places Humboltia dura within the broader group of plants known as the dogbanes or oleander family. Further, it belongs to the genus Humboldtia, which consists of evergreen trees and shrubs native to tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Humboltia dura is also supported by its botanical characteristics and the presence of milky sap, a trait common in Apocynaceae plants. This classification system helps to categorize and understand the diversity of plant species within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Humboltia dura, also known as the Dura, is a plant native to parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine.
